title: unfiltered chef xabier goikoetxea
full_text: Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Now Reading: Unfiltered: Chef Xabier Goikoetxea Beyond The Pass 1 01 Unfiltered: Chef Xabier Goikoetxea Beyond The Pass Main// Eat// Drink// Reviews// Insider// At Home// About Us// Download Our Magazine// Q2 2026// Q1 2026// Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026// Home Insider Unfiltered: Chef Xabier Goikoetxea Beyond The PassUnfiltered: Chef Xabier Goikoetxea Beyond The PassPatrick LohInsider9 months agoShare From wild kitchen tales to guilty pleasures, the Spanish chef lifts the lid on life outside the line. Seven restaurants. Twelve Michelin stars. Countless lessons learned. Chef Xabier Goikoetxea—protégé of Spain’s legendary Martín Berasategui and the creative force at Oria restaurant in Barcelona—has spent his career mastering precision, flavour, and discipline. But behind the gleaming kitchen doors lies a personality that is far more spontaneous and playful than one might expect. In this episode of Unfiltered, Chef Goikoetxea takes us past the polished plates and into the stories that shaped him: the chaos of his wildest kitchen moments, the comfort foods he secretly craves, and the unexpected inspirations that continue to drive his culinary vision. At the heart of it all is Barcelona—its rhythm, energy, and soul—fueling both the chef and the cuisine that carries his signature. Craziest kitchen moment Chef Xabier Goikoetxea at Oria by Martín Berasategui. “My first day at Martín Berasategui’s restaurant was pure chaos, in the best way,” recalls Chef Goikoetxea. “I had finally stepped into a true, world-class kitchen, where the best chefs came together to create flavours at the highest level.” For context: Chef Berasategui, the Basque master based in Lasarte-Oria, has held three Michelin stars since 2001 and a staggering twelve in total. That’s more than any other Spanish chef. Untouchable dish Donostiarra soup. “One dish I would never change is the Donostiarra soup. To me, it’s the best soup in the world,” he insists. This Basque classic from San Sebastián is a deeply flavoured seafood broth made with monkfish, hake, prawns, clams, and vegetables. It’s rich, silky, and unapologetically soulful. Most loved ingredient Tomato Illusion Macaron. “I can’t live without Piment d’Espelette powder,” he says. “It’s a special Basque pepper I use in so many of my dishes. It adds a kick, but with nuance—fruity, smoky, slightly sweet. It’s never about burning heat; it’s about depth.” Love to hate Turbot, one of Chef Goikoetxea’s favourite dishes. “Fast food,” he admits bluntly. “Too much sugar, salt, saturated fat, preservatives—too little of what food should really be.” Happiest moment Chef Xabier assembling the Comté Cheese Crunch. Winning a Michelin star for Oria remains his proudest milestone. “It was pure joy and motivation for the entire team,” says Chef Goikoetxea. “More than just recognition, it was proof that we’re doing something right in the culinary world.” From guilty pleasures to culinary passions, the full Unfiltered interview awaits.
